message:
feat(web): Complete WebSocket auto-reconnect integration

- Add reconnect.ts backoff utilities (was missing from prior commit)
- Implement full WebSocketProvider with reconnection lifecycle
- Add ConnectionStatusIndicator with clickable manual reconnect
- Support channel subscriptions, message queueing, and re-subscription

The WebSocket context now provides:
- Exponential backoff with full jitter for reconnection
- Max retry threshold with manual reconnect fallback
- Real-time status events for UI feedback
- Message queue for offline operation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-5nm","title":"File Reservation System","description":"## Background

The File Reservation System prevents file conflicts when multiple agents work in the same codebase. Without coordination, agents might simultaneously modify the same file, leading to merge conflicts, lost work, or inconsistent state.

This system provides glob-based file reservations with two modes:
- **Exclusive**: Only the reservation holder can modify matching files
- **Shared**: Multiple agents can read, one can write with coordination

Reservations are TTL-based to prevent deadlocks from crashed or stuck agents.

## Technical Architecture

### Reservation Model
```typescript
interface FileReservation {
  id: string;                    // UUID
  projectId: string;             // Project scope
  agentId: string;               // Reserving agent
  patterns: string[];            // Glob patterns
  mode: 'exclusive' | 'shared';  // Reservation type
  ttl: number;                   // Time-to-live in seconds
  createdAt: Date;
  expiresAt: Date;
  renewCount: number;            // Number of renewals
  metadata: {
    reason?: string;             // Why reserved
    taskId?: string;             // Associated task
  };
}
```

### Glob Pattern Matching
Uses `micromatch` for fast, accurate glob matching:
```typescript
// Example patterns
'src/**/*.ts'           // All TypeScript in src
'!src/**/*.test.ts'     // Exclude tests
'packages/core/src/*'   // Direct children only
'**/{package,tsconfig}.json'  // Specific files anywhere
```

### Core Operations

#### 1. Create Reservation
```typescript
async createReservation(params: {
  projectId: string;
  agentId: string;
  patterns: string[];
  mode: 'exclusive' | 'shared';
  ttl?: number;          // Default: 300 (5 minutes)
  reason?: string;
}): Promise<{
  reservation: FileReservation;
  conflicts: ConflictInfo[];  // Empty if granted
  granted: boolean;
}>
```

#### 2. Check Reservation
```typescript
async checkReservation(params: {
  projectId: string;
  agentId: string;
  filePath: string;
}): Promise<{
  allowed: boolean;
  heldBy?: string;        // Agent holding reservation
  expiresAt?: Date;
  mode?: 'exclusive' | 'shared';
}>
```

#### 3. Release Reservation
```typescript
async releaseReservation(params: {
  reservationId: string;
  agentId: string;        // Must match holder
}): Promise<{ released: boolean }>
```

#### 4. Renew Reservation
```typescript
async renewReservation(params: {
  reservationId: string;
  agentId: string;
  additionalTtl?: number; // Default: original TTL
}): Promise<{
  renewed: boolean;
  newExpiresAt: Date;
}>
```

#### 5. List Reservations
```typescript
async listReservations(params: {
  projectId: string;
  agentId?: string;       // Filter by agent
  filePath?: string;      // Filter by affected file
}): Promise<FileReservation[]>
```

### Conflict Detection Algorithm

When a new reservation is requested:
1. Expand all patterns to a set of potential file paths
2. For each existing reservation in the project:
   a. Expand its patterns
   b. Compute intersection with new patterns
   c. If intersection non-empty AND modes conflict, record conflict
3. Return conflicts or grant reservation

```typescript
function detectConflicts(
  newPatterns: string[],
  newMode: ReservationMode,
  existingReservations: FileReservation[]
): ConflictInfo[] {
  const conflicts: ConflictInfo[] = [];
  
  for (const existing of existingReservations) {
    const overlap = computePatternOverlap(newPatterns, existing.patterns);
    
    if (overlap.length > 0) {
      // Exclusive conflicts with everything
      // Shared only conflicts with exclusive
      if (existing.mode === 'exclusive' || newMode === 'exclusive') {
        conflicts.push({
          reservationId: existing.id,
          agentId: existing.agentId,
          overlappingPatterns: overlap,
          expiresAt: existing.expiresAt
        });
      }
    }
  }
  
  return conflicts;
}
```

### TTL Management

- Background job runs every 10 seconds
- Expired reservations are marked and cleaned
- Agents can subscribe to expiration warnings (30s before)
- Maximum TTL: 1 hour (configurable)
- Maximum renewals: 10 (prevents indefinite locks)

## UI Components

### Reservation Map View
Visual representation of current reservations:
```
Project: flywheel-gateway
==========================

[Agent: architect-001] (exclusive, expires in 4:32)
  - src/services/**/*.ts
  - src/models/**/*.ts

[Agent: coder-002] (shared, expires in 2:15)
  - src/utils/*.ts
  - src/helpers/*.ts

[Agent: tester-003] (exclusive, expires in 8:45)
  - **/*.test.ts
  - **/*.spec.ts

Conflicts: None
```

### Real-time Updates
- WebSocket subscription for reservation changes
- Live conflict notifications
- Expiration countdown timers

## File Locations

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `apps/gateway/src/services/reservation.service.ts` | Core reservation logic |
| `apps/gateway/src/services/reservation.types.ts` | TypeScript interfaces |
| `apps/gateway/src/services/reservation.store.ts` | Storage abstraction |
| `apps/gateway/src/services/pattern-matcher.ts` | Glob pattern utilities |
| `apps/gateway/src/controllers/reservation.controller.ts` | REST API endpoints |
| `apps/gateway/src/websocket/reservation.gateway.ts` | WebSocket handlers |
| `apps/gateway/src/jobs/reservation-cleanup.job.ts` | TTL enforcement |
| `apps/web/src/components/ReservationMap.tsx` | UI component |
| `apps/gateway/src/__tests__/reservation.service.test.ts` | Unit tests |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Create, check, release, renew, list operations
- [ ] Glob pattern matching with negation support
- [ ] Exclusive vs shared mode semantics
- [ ] Conflict detection for overlapping patterns
- [ ] TTL-based automatic expiration
- [ ] Maximum renewal limit enforcement
- [ ] REST API endpoints for all operations
- [ ] WebSocket events for real-time updates
- [ ] Reservation map UI component
- [ ] Background cleanup job for expired reservations
- [ ] >95% test coverage for conflict detection
- [ ] Performance: <50ms for conflict check with 100 active reservations
- [ ] Documentation with usage examples

## References

- PLAN.md Section 11: File Coordination
- PLAN.md Section 12: Conflict Prevention

## Dependencies

- `micromatch` - Glob pattern matching
- `ioredis` - Redis for distributed state
- `cron` - Background job scheduling
- Socket.io for WebSocket support

## Edge Cases to Handle

1. Agent requests pattern that is subset of own existing reservation
2. Agent crashes without releasing reservation
3. Two agents request overlapping patterns simultaneously
4. Pattern expansion exceeds memory limits (cap at 10,000 files)
5. Reservation renewal race conditions
6. Project deletion with active reservations


## Testing Requirements

Reference: `flywheel_gateway-d8b`.

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Reservation conflict detection matches symmetric glob semantics
- [ ] TTL expiry and renew semantics behave correctly (extend from max(now, expires))
- [ ] Exclusive vs shared reservation rules are enforced deterministically

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Create/renew/release reservations against Agent Mail (mock or local) and verify server state
- [ ] Concurrent reservation attempts for overlapping patterns resolve correctly (one conflict, one granted)

### Failure Mode Tests
- [ ] Agent Mail unavailable → graceful degradation with actionable error + no local corruption

### E2E Tests
- [ ] Covered by `flywheel_gateway-tz4` critical-path E2E suite; add a focused E2E spec if this feature introduces unique user workflows
- [ ] Failure mode E2E validates actionable recovery UI/messages (with artifacts captured)

### Logging
- [ ] Logs include correlationId + reservationId + pathPattern + exclusive + expiresTs

### Failure Mode Tests

- [ ] Invalid glob pattern: validation error
- [ ] Reserve expired agent: error
- [ ] Concurrent reserve race: handled correctly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-08T13:38:37.14682048-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:11:25.374662508-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:11:25.374662508-05:00","close_reason":"File Reservation System fully implemented with core service, REST API, improved glob matching, and comprehensive tests (39 passing)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_gateway-5nm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_gateway-61i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-08T14:01:46.297929756-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_gateway-5nm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_gateway-6mn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-08T14:01:47.21369042-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-5nq","title":"FEAT: DCG Integration (Destructive Command Guard)","description":"## Background

DCG (Destructive Command Guard) is an external Rust binary and shell hook that **mechanically enforces command safety** at the exact execution boundary. It blocks dangerous commands (or warns) *before* they run, with very low latency.

Flywheel Gateway’s job is **integration and visibility**, not re-implementing DCG.

- DCG already blocks: destructive git ops, dangerous filesystem ops, risky DB commands, etc.
- Gateway must surface DCG decisions in the agent UX, preserve an audit trail, and provide UI to manage allowlists/config.

## Goals

1. **Visibility**: show DCG blocks/warnings inline in agent output and in a dedicated dashboard.
2. **Governance**: manage per-project / per-agent allowlists and enabled packs via UI + REST.
3. **Safety workflow**: route high-severity blocks into the SLB approval queue; critical blocks are always denied.
4. **Learning loop**: capture false-positive feedback and feed it into CM (Cass-Memory) to reduce repeats.

## Non-Goals

- Rebuilding DCG’s pack/pattern engine in TypeScript.
- Providing a bypass mechanism that defeats DCG’s mechanical enforcement.

## Data Model (source of truth)

```ts
// packages/shared/src/types/dcg.ts

export interface DCGBlockEvent {
  id: string;
  timestamp: Date;
  agentId: string;
  command: string;
  pack: string; // e.g., "core.git", "database.postgresql"
  pattern: string;
  ruleId: string; // allowlist key
  severity: 'critical' | 'high' | 'medium' | 'low';
  reason: string;
  contextClassification: 'executed' | 'data' | 'ambiguous';
  falsePositive?: boolean;
  allowlisted?: boolean;
}

export interface DCGAllowlistEntry {
  ruleId: string;
  pattern: string;
  addedAt: Date;
  addedBy: string;
  reason: string;
  expiresAt?: Date;
  condition?: string; // e.g. "CI=true"
}

export interface DCGConfig {
  enabledPacks: string[];
  disabledPacks: string[];
  allowlist: DCGAllowlistEntry[];
}

export interface DCGStats {
  totalBlocks: number;
  blocksByPack: Record<string, number>;
  blocksBySeverity: Record<string, number>;
  falsePositiveRate: number;
  topBlockedCommands: Array<{ command: string; count: number }>;
}
```

## Integration Architecture

Gateway integrates DCG in three places:

1. **Agent execution boundary** (AgentDriver)
   - When agents execute commands, DCG may emit block/warn output.
   - Gateway must detect DCG outcomes and convert them into structured `DCGBlockEvent` records.

2. **Storage + API** (Gateway backend)
   - Persist block events.
   - Expose REST endpoints for config, allowlists, packs, history, and stats.

3. **Realtime + UI**
   - Publish `dcg.block` / `dcg.warn` events via the WebSocket layer (flywheel_gateway-46c).
   - Provide a dashboard to view recent blocks, stats, pack configuration, and allowlist management.

### REST API Endpoints (PLAN.md §17.6.3)

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/dcg/config` | Get DCG configuration |
| `PUT` | `/dcg/config` | Update DCG configuration |
| `GET` | `/dcg/packs` | List available packs |
| `POST` | `/dcg/packs/{pack}/enable` | Enable a pack |
| `POST` | `/dcg/packs/{pack}/disable` | Disable a pack |
| `GET` | `/dcg/blocks` | List block history |
| `POST` | `/dcg/blocks/{id}/false-positive` | Mark as false positive |
| `GET` | `/dcg/allowlist` | List allowlist entries |
| `POST` | `/dcg/allowlist` | Add allowlist entry |
| `DELETE` | `/dcg/allowlist/{ruleId}` | Remove allowlist entry |
| `GET` | `/dcg/stats` | Get block statistics |

### WebSocket Events (PLAN.md §17.6.4)

```ts
interface DCGEvent {
  type: 'dcg.block' | 'dcg.warn' | 'dcg.allowlist_added' | 'dcg.false_positive';
  data: DCGBlockEvent | DCGAllowlistEntry;
}
```

## UX Requirements

- DCG blocks must be visually distinct in output streams (severity badge + reason + ruleId).
- UI must make it obvious when something is **not allowlistable** (e.g., severity `critical`).
- Allowlist actions must require an explicit reason and should be audited.
- False-positive marking must be one click but recorded with actor + timestamp.

## Safety / Security Considerations

- Redact secrets in `command` (and any captured context).
- Never log OAuth tokens / API keys / secrets.
- Pagination and filtering required on `/dcg/blocks`.
- Any config change should be versioned and auditable.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Gateway can ingest DCG block/warn outcomes into `DCGBlockEvent` with stable schema.
- [ ] `/dcg/*` endpoints exist and are consistent with the shared types.
- [ ] WebSocket publishes dcg events to subscribed clients.
- [ ] UI dashboard shows recent blocks + stats and supports allowlist + false-positive flows.
- [ ] SLB integration: `high` severity blocks can be queued for approval; `critical` is always denied.
- [ ] Missing/disabled DCG degrades gracefully (feature reported unavailable; no crashes).

## Testing Requirements

Reference: `flywheel_gateway-d8b`.

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Block event parsing validates schema and applies redaction/sanitization rules
- [ ] Severity + allowlist rules are deterministic for the same event inputs
- [ ] Config update validation prevents invalid pack names and invalid allowlist entries

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Ingest a representative block event → persisted + exposed via REST + emitted via WS
- [ ] Allowlist add/remove → reflected in config response and audited

### E2E Tests (UI)
- [ ] DCG block appears in dashboard with sanitized command preview and correct severity badge
- [ ] Allowlist flow requires a reason and updates stats

### Logging
- [ ] Logs include correlationId + dcgEventId + severity + pack + ruleId; secrets are never logged

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-5sq","title":"DCG: Implement Pending Exceptions (Allow-Once)","description":"## Problem Statement

DCG supports "pending exceptions" (allow-once workflow) where users can approve a blocked command for single execution using a short code. This enables users to bypass false positives without permanently allowlisting dangerous patterns.

## Background

DCG's pending exception workflow:
1. DCG blocks a command and generates a short code (e.g., "abc123")
2. User reviews the blocked command and decides it's safe
3. User runs `dcg allow abc123` to approve single execution
4. DCG allows that specific command+SHA256 hash once
5. If same command runs again, it requires new approval

### Current State

The Gateway has no implementation for this workflow. Users must:
- SSH to server directly
- Run CLI commands manually
- No visibility into pending exceptions

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Database Schema

```typescript
// apps/gateway/src/db/schema.ts

export const dcgPendingExceptions = sqliteTable("dcg_pending_exceptions", {
  id: text("id").primaryKey(),
  shortCode: text("short_code").unique().notNull(), // e.g., "abc123"

  // Command details
  command: text("command").notNull(),           // The blocked command
  commandHash: text("command_hash").notNull(),  // SHA256 for verification

  // Rule that triggered block
  pack: text("pack").notNull(),
  ruleId: text("rule_id").notNull(),
  reason: text("reason").notNull(),
  severity: text("severity").notNull(),

  // Context
  agentId: text("agent_id"),
  blockEventId: text("block_event_id"),         // Reference to dcgBlocks entry

  // Status
  status: text("status").default("pending"),    // pending|approved|denied|expired
  approvedBy: text("approved_by"),
  approvedAt: integer("approved_at", { mode: "timestamp" }),
  deniedBy: text("denied_by"),
  deniedAt: integer("denied_at", { mode: "timestamp" }),
  denyReason: text("deny_reason"),

  // Execution tracking
  executedAt: integer("executed_at", { mode: "timestamp" }),
  executionResult: text("execution_result"),    // success|failed

  // Timestamps
  createdAt: integer("created_at", { mode: "timestamp" }).default(sql`(unixepoch())`),
  expiresAt: integer("expires_at", { mode: "timestamp" }).notNull(), // Short TTL
}, (table) => ({
  shortCodeIdx: index("dcg_pending_short_code_idx").on(table.shortCode),
  statusIdx: index("dcg_pending_status_idx").on(table.status),
  agentIdx: index("dcg_pending_agent_idx").on(table.agentId),
  expiresIdx: index("dcg_pending_expires_idx").on(table.expiresAt),
}));
```

### 2. Service Layer

```typescript
// apps/gateway/src/services/dcg-pending.service.ts

import { nanoid } from "nanoid";
import { createHash } from "crypto";
import { db } from "../db";
import { dcgPendingExceptions, dcgBlocks } from "../db/schema";
import { getHub } from "../ws/hub";
import { logger } from "./logger";

interface CreatePendingExceptionParams {
  command: string;
  pack: string;
  ruleId: string;
  reason: string;
  severity: string;
  agentId?: string;
  blockEventId?: string;
  ttlSeconds?: number;
}

export async function createPendingException(
  params: CreatePendingExceptionParams
): Promise<PendingException> {
  const correlationId = getCorrelationId();
  const startTime = Date.now();

  const shortCode = generateShortCode();
  const commandHash = hashCommand(params.command);
  const expiresAt = new Date(Date.now() + (params.ttlSeconds || 300) * 1000);

  const exception = {
    id: generateId("dcg_pend_"),
    shortCode,
    command: params.command,
    commandHash,
    pack: params.pack,
    ruleId: params.ruleId,
    reason: params.reason,
    severity: params.severity,
    agentId: params.agentId,
    blockEventId: params.blockEventId,
    status: "pending",
    expiresAt,
  };

  await db.insert(dcgPendingExceptions).values(exception);

  // Publish event for real-time UI updates
  getHub().publish(
    { kind: "system", scope: "dcg" },
    {
      type: "dcg.pending_exception_created",
      data: { shortCode, command: redactSensitive(params.command), expiresAt },
      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
    }
  );

  logger.info({
    correlationId,
    duration_ms: Date.now() - startTime,
    shortCode,
    ruleId: params.ruleId,
    expiresAt: expiresAt.toISOString(),
  }, "Created pending exception");

  return exception;
}

export async function approvePendingException(
  shortCode: string,
  approvedBy: string
): Promise<PendingException> {
  const correlationId = getCorrelationId();

  const exception = await db.select()
    .from(dcgPendingExceptions)
    .where(eq(dcgPendingExceptions.shortCode, shortCode))
    .get();

  if (!exception) {
    throw new NotFoundError(`Pending exception not found: ${shortCode}`);
  }

  if (exception.status !== "pending") {
    throw new ConflictError(`Exception already ${exception.status}`);
  }

  if (new Date(exception.expiresAt) < new Date()) {
    throw new ExpiredError(`Exception expired at ${exception.expiresAt}`);
  }

  await db.update(dcgPendingExceptions)
    .set({
      status: "approved",
      approvedBy,
      approvedAt: sql`(unixepoch())`,
    })
    .where(eq(dcgPendingExceptions.id, exception.id));

  // Publish approval event
  getHub().publish(
    { kind: "system", scope: "dcg" },
    {
      type: "dcg.pending_exception_approved",
      data: { shortCode, approvedBy },
      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
    }
  );

  logger.info({ correlationId, shortCode, approvedBy }, "Approved pending exception");

  return { ...exception, status: "approved", approvedBy };
}

export async function denyPendingException(
  shortCode: string,
  deniedBy: string,
  reason?: string
): Promise<PendingException> {
  const exception = await db.select()
    .from(dcgPendingExceptions)
    .where(eq(dcgPendingExceptions.shortCode, shortCode))
    .get();

  if (!exception) {
    throw new NotFoundError(`Pending exception not found: ${shortCode}`);
  }

  await db.update(dcgPendingExceptions)
    .set({
      status: "denied",
      deniedBy,
      deniedAt: sql`(unixepoch())`,
      denyReason: reason,
    })
    .where(eq(dcgPendingExceptions.id, exception.id));

  getHub().publish(
    { kind: "system", scope: "dcg" },
    {
      type: "dcg.pending_exception_denied",
      data: { shortCode, deniedBy, reason },
      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
    }
  );

  return { ...exception, status: "denied", deniedBy };
}

export async function validateExceptionForExecution(
  commandHash: string
): Promise<PendingException | null> {
  const exception = await db.select()
    .from(dcgPendingExceptions)
    .where(
      and(
        eq(dcgPendingExceptions.commandHash, commandHash),
        eq(dcgPendingExceptions.status, "approved"),
        gt(dcgPendingExceptions.expiresAt, sql`(unixepoch())`)
      )
    )
    .get();

  return exception || null;
}

export async function markExceptionExecuted(
  id: string,
  result: "success" | "failed"
): Promise<void> {
  await db.update(dcgPendingExceptions)
    .set({
      executedAt: sql`(unixepoch())`,
      executionResult: result,
      status: "executed",
    })
    .where(eq(dcgPendingExceptions.id, id));
}

export async function cleanupExpiredExceptions(): Promise<number> {
  const result = await db.update(dcgPendingExceptions)
    .set({ status: "expired" })
    .where(
      and(
        eq(dcgPendingExceptions.status, "pending"),
        lt(dcgPendingExceptions.expiresAt, sql`(unixepoch())`)
      )
    );

  logger.info({ expiredCount: result.changes }, "Cleaned up expired pending exceptions");
  return result.changes;
}

function generateShortCode(): string {
  return nanoid(6).toLowerCase();
}

function hashCommand(command: string): string {
  return createHash("sha256").update(command).digest("hex");
}

function redactSensitive(command: string): string {
  // Redact potential secrets in command display
  return command.replace(/(password|secret|token|key)=[^\s]+/gi, "$1=***");
}
```

### 3. REST API Routes

```typescript
// routes/dcg.ts - Add pending exception endpoints

// GET /dcg/pending - List pending exceptions
dcg.get("/pending", async (c) => {
  const status = c.req.query("status") || "pending";
  const agentId = c.req.query("agentId");
  const limit = Number(c.req.query("limit")) || 50;

  let query = db.select()
    .from(dcgPendingExceptions)
    .orderBy(desc(dcgPendingExceptions.createdAt))
    .limit(limit);

  if (status) {
    query = query.where(eq(dcgPendingExceptions.status, status));
  }
  if (agentId) {
    query = query.where(eq(dcgPendingExceptions.agentId, agentId));
  }

  const exceptions = await query;
  return c.json({ exceptions });
});

// GET /dcg/pending/:shortCode - Get specific exception
dcg.get("/pending/:shortCode", async (c) => {
  const { shortCode } = c.req.param();
  const exception = await db.select()
    .from(dcgPendingExceptions)
    .where(eq(dcgPendingExceptions.shortCode, shortCode))
    .get();

  if (!exception) {
    return c.json({ error: "Not found" }, 404);
  }
  return c.json(exception);
});

// POST /dcg/pending/:shortCode/approve - Approve exception
dcg.post("/pending/:shortCode/approve", async (c) => {
  const { shortCode } = c.req.param();
  const user = c.get("user") || "anonymous";

  try {
    const exception = await approvePendingException(shortCode, user);
    return c.json({ success: true, exception });
  } catch (error) {
    if (error instanceof NotFoundError) {
      return c.json({ error: error.message }, 404);
    }
    if (error instanceof ExpiredError) {
      return c.json({ error: error.message }, 410);
    }
    throw error;
  }
});

// POST /dcg/pending/:shortCode/deny - Deny exception
dcg.post("/pending/:shortCode/deny", async (c) => {
  const { shortCode } = c.req.param();
  const user = c.get("user") || "anonymous";
  const body = await c.req.json().catch(() => ({}));

  try {
    const exception = await denyPendingException(shortCode, user, body.reason);
    return c.json({ success: true, exception });
  } catch (error) {
    if (error instanceof NotFoundError) {
      return c.json({ error: error.message }, 404);
    }
    throw error;
  }
});

// POST /dcg/pending/:shortCode/validate - Validate for execution
dcg.post("/pending/:shortCode/validate", async (c) => {
  const { shortCode } = c.req.param();
  const body = await c.req.json();

  const exception = await db.select()
    .from(dcgPendingExceptions)
    .where(eq(dcgPendingExceptions.shortCode, shortCode))
    .get();

  if (!exception) {
    return c.json({ valid: false, reason: "Not found" });
  }

  if (exception.status !== "approved") {
    return c.json({ valid: false, reason: `Status is ${exception.status}` });
  }

  if (exception.commandHash !== body.commandHash) {
    return c.json({ valid: false, reason: "Command hash mismatch" });
  }

  if (new Date(exception.expiresAt) < new Date()) {
    return c.json({ valid: false, reason: "Expired" });
  }

  return c.json({ valid: true, exception });
});
```

### 4. Periodic Cleanup Job

```typescript
// apps/gateway/src/jobs/dcg-cleanup.ts

import { Cron } from "croner";
import { cleanupExpiredExceptions } from "../services/dcg-pending.service";
import { logger } from "../services/logger";

// Run every minute to clean up expired exceptions
export const dcgCleanupJob = new Cron("* * * * *", async () => {
  const correlationId = generateCorrelationId();
  const startTime = Date.now();

  try {
    const expiredCount = await cleanupExpiredExceptions();

    if (expiredCount > 0) {
      logger.info({
        correlationId,
        duration_ms: Date.now() - startTime,
        expiredCount,
      }, "DCG cleanup job completed");
    }
  } catch (error) {
    logger.error({ correlationId, error }, "DCG cleanup job failed");
  }
});
```

## File Locations

- `apps/gateway/src/db/schema.ts` - Add dcgPendingExceptions table
- `apps/gateway/src/services/dcg-pending.service.ts` - Pending exception service
- `apps/gateway/src/routes/dcg.ts` - REST API endpoints
- `apps/gateway/src/jobs/dcg-cleanup.ts` - Expiration cleanup job

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (`apps/gateway/tests/unit/dcg-pending.test.ts`)

```typescript
describe("DCG Pending Exceptions Service", () => {
  beforeEach(async () => {
    await clearPendingExceptions();
    logger.info({ testName: "beforeEach" }, "Cleared pending exceptions");
  });

  describe("createPendingException", () => {
    it("should create exception with short code", async () => {
      const startTime = Date.now();
      const exception = await createPendingException({
        command: "dangerous-command --flag",
        pack: "core.git",
        ruleId: "core.git:dangerous",
        reason: "Potentially dangerous",
        severity: "high",
      });

      expect(exception.shortCode).toMatch(/^[a-z0-9]{6}$/);
      expect(exception.status).toBe("pending");
      expect(exception.commandHash).toHaveLength(64); // SHA256

      logger.info({
        testName: "create_with_short_code",
        duration_ms: Date.now() - startTime,
        shortCode: exception.shortCode,
        commandHash: exception.commandHash,
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "Created pending exception");
    });

    it("should set expiration time based on TTL", async () => {
      const exception = await createPendingException({
        command: "test-command",
        pack: "test",
        ruleId: "test:rule",
        reason: "Test",
        severity: "low",
        ttlSeconds: 60,
      });

      const expectedExpiry = new Date(Date.now() + 60 * 1000);
      const actualExpiry = new Date(exception.expiresAt);

      // Allow 1 second tolerance
      expect(Math.abs(actualExpiry.getTime() - expectedExpiry.getTime())).toBeLessThan(1000);

      logger.info({
        testName: "expiration_ttl",
        ttlSeconds: 60,
        expiresAt: exception.expiresAt,
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "Verified expiration time");
    });

    it("should publish WebSocket event on creation", async () => {
      const events: any[] = [];
      const unsubscribe = mockHub.subscribe("system:dcg", (e) => events.push(e));

      await createPendingException({
        command: "test-command",
        pack: "test",
        ruleId: "test:rule",
        reason: "Test",
        severity: "low",
      });

      expect(events).toHaveLength(1);
      expect(events[0].type).toBe("dcg.pending_exception_created");

      unsubscribe();
      logger.info({
        testName: "websocket_event",
        eventCount: events.length,
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "WebSocket event published");
    });
  });

  describe("approvePendingException", () => {
    it("should approve pending exception", async () => {
      const exception = await createPendingException({
        command: "test-command",
        pack: "test",
        ruleId: "test:rule",
        reason: "Test",
        severity: "low",
      });

      const approved = await approvePendingException(exception.shortCode, "test-user");

      expect(approved.status).toBe("approved");
      expect(approved.approvedBy).toBe("test-user");

      logger.info({
        testName: "approve_exception",
        shortCode: exception.shortCode,
        approvedBy: approved.approvedBy,
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "Approved pending exception");
    });

    it("should throw NotFoundError for unknown short code", async () => {
      await expect(
        approvePendingException("invalid", "test-user")
      ).rejects.toThrow(NotFoundError);

      logger.info({
        testName: "approve_not_found",
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "NotFoundError thrown for invalid code");
    });

    it("should throw ExpiredError for expired exception", async () => {
      const exception = await createPendingException({
        command: "test-command",
        pack: "test",
        ruleId: "test:rule",
        reason: "Test",
        severity: "low",
        ttlSeconds: 1,
      });

      // Wait for expiration
      await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1100));

      await expect(
        approvePendingException(exception.shortCode, "test-user")
      ).rejects.toThrow(ExpiredError);

      logger.info({
        testName: "approve_expired",
        shortCode: exception.shortCode,
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "ExpiredError thrown for expired exception");
    });
  });

  describe("validateExceptionForExecution", () => {
    it("should return approved exception for matching hash", async () => {
      const exception = await createPendingException({
        command: "test-command",
        pack: "test",
        ruleId: "test:rule",
        reason: "Test",
        severity: "low",
      });

      await approvePendingException(exception.shortCode, "test-user");

      const valid = await validateExceptionForExecution(exception.commandHash);

      expect(valid).not.toBeNull();
      expect(valid!.shortCode).toBe(exception.shortCode);

      logger.info({
        testName: "validate_approved",
        shortCode: exception.shortCode,
        valid: !!valid,
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "Validated approved exception");
    });

    it("should return null for unapproved exception", async () => {
      const exception = await createPendingException({
        command: "test-command",
        pack: "test",
        ruleId: "test:rule",
        reason: "Test",
        severity: "low",
      });

      const valid = await validateExceptionForExecution(exception.commandHash);

      expect(valid).toBeNull();

      logger.info({
        testName: "validate_unapproved",
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "Unapproved exception returned null");
    });
  });

  describe("cleanupExpiredExceptions", () => {
    it("should mark expired exceptions", async () => {
      // Create exception that expires immediately
      await createPendingException({
        command: "test-command",
        pack: "test",
        ruleId: "test:rule",
        reason: "Test",
        severity: "low",
        ttlSeconds: 0,
      });

      // Wait a tick
      await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));

      const expiredCount = await cleanupExpiredExceptions();

      expect(expiredCount).toBe(1);

      logger.info({
        testName: "cleanup_expired",
        expiredCount,
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "Cleaned up expired exceptions");
    });
  });
});
```

### Integration Tests (`apps/gateway/tests/integration/dcg-pending.test.ts`)

```typescript
describe("DCG Pending Exceptions Integration", () => {
  it("should complete full approval workflow", async () => {
    // Create exception from block event
    const blockEvent = await ingestBlockEvent({
      command: "test command",
      pack: "test.pack",
      pattern: "test",
      ruleId: "test:rule",
      severity: "medium",
      reason: "Test block",
    });

    const exception = await createPendingException({
      command: "test command",
      pack: "test.pack",
      ruleId: "test:rule",
      reason: "Test block",
      severity: "medium",
      blockEventId: blockEvent.id,
    });

    // Approve
    await approvePendingException(exception.shortCode, "integration-test");

    // Validate for execution
    const valid = await validateExceptionForExecution(exception.commandHash);
    expect(valid).not.toBeNull();

    // Mark executed
    await markExceptionExecuted(exception.id, "success");

    // Verify final state
    const final = await db.select()
      .from(dcgPendingExceptions)
      .where(eq(dcgPendingExceptions.id, exception.id))
      .get();

    expect(final.status).toBe("executed");
    expect(final.executionResult).toBe("success");

    logger.info({
      testName: "full_approval_workflow",
      exceptionId: exception.id,
      blockEventId: blockEvent.id,
      finalStatus: final.status,
      correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
    }, "Full approval workflow completed");
  });
});
```

### E2E Tests (`apps/gateway/tests/e2e/dcg-pending.test.ts`)

```typescript
describe("DCG Pending Exceptions E2E", () => {
  it("should approve via REST API and receive WebSocket notification", async () => {
    // Create pending exception
    const exception = await createPendingException({
      command: "test e2e command",
      pack: "test",
      ruleId: "test:e2e",
      reason: "E2E test",
      severity: "low",
    });

    // Connect WebSocket
    const ws = await connectWebSocket("/ws");
    await ws.subscribe("system:dcg");

    // Approve via API
    const response = await fetch(`/dcg/pending/${exception.shortCode}/approve`, {
      method: "POST",
    });

    expect(response.status).toBe(200);

    // Wait for event
    const event = await ws.waitForEvent("dcg.pending_exception_approved", 5000);
    expect(event.data.shortCode).toBe(exception.shortCode);

    logger.info({
      testName: "e2e_approve_websocket",
      shortCode: exception.shortCode,
      responseStatus: response.status,
      correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
    }, "E2E approval with WebSocket notification");

    await ws.close();
  });

  it("should list pending exceptions in UI", async () => {
    // Create multiple exceptions
    await Promise.all([
      createPendingException({ command: "cmd1", pack: "test", ruleId: "test:1", reason: "Test 1", severity: "low" }),
      createPendingException({ command: "cmd2", pack: "test", ruleId: "test:2", reason: "Test 2", severity: "medium" }),
      createPendingException({ command: "cmd3", pack: "test", ruleId: "test:3", reason: "Test 3", severity: "high" }),
    ]);

    const response = await fetch("/dcg/pending?status=pending");
    const { exceptions } = await response.json();

    expect(exceptions.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);

    logger.info({
      testName: "e2e_list_pending",
      exceptionCount: exceptions.length,
      correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
    }, "E2E list pending exceptions");
  });
});
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pending exceptions have unique short codes (6 chars, lowercase alphanumeric)
- [ ] Exceptions expire after configured TTL (default 5 minutes)
- [ ] Approve endpoint updates status and records approver
- [ ] Deny endpoint updates status and records denier with reason
- [ ] Validate endpoint verifies command hash matches
- [ ] WebSocket events published for create/approve/deny
- [ ] Cleanup job expires stale pending exceptions
- [ ] All unit tests pass with comprehensive logging
- [ ] All integration tests pass
- [ ] All E2E tests pass

## Security Considerations

- Short codes are not guessable (6 chars = 2^30+ combinations)
- Command hash verified before execution allowed
- Approval requires authenticated user
- All approvals/denials are audit logged
- Expired exceptions cannot be used

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-61i","title":"Agent Mail Integration (MCP Client)","description":"## Background

Agent Mail provides MCP-based (Model Context Protocol) inter-agent communication within the Flywheel ecosystem. This enables agents to collaborate, delegate tasks, share context, and coordinate their activities without requiring direct connections.

The mail system uses a message queue architecture where agents send messages to named mailboxes. This decouples senders from receivers and enables asynchronous communication patterns essential for multi-agent orchestration.

## Technical Architecture

### MCP Integration
Agent Mail is exposed as an MCP server that agents connect to as clients. This leverages the standard MCP tool-calling mechanism for all mail operations:

```typescript
// MCP Tools exposed by Agent Mail
tools: [
  'agentmail_ensure_project',    // Create/verify project exists
  'agentmail_register_agent',    // Register agent in project
  'agentmail_send_message',      // Send message to agent
  'agentmail_reply',             // Reply to received message
  'agentmail_fetch_inbox',       // Get pending messages
  'agentmail_request_file_reservation'  // Request file lock
]
```

### Message Structure
```typescript
interface AgentMailMessage {
  id: string;                    // UUID
  projectId: string;             // Project context
  from: AgentIdentity;           // Sender agent
  to: AgentIdentity;             // Recipient agent
  subject: string;               // Message subject
  body: any;                     // Structured content (JSON)
  replyTo?: string;              // Parent message ID for threads
  priority: 'low' | 'normal' | 'high' | 'urgent';
  ttl: number;                   // Time-to-live in seconds
  metadata: Record<string, any>; // Extension point
  createdAt: Date;
  expiresAt: Date;
}
```

### Core Operations

#### 1. Ensure Project
Idempotently creates a project context for agent communication:
```typescript
agentmail_ensure_project({
  projectId: string,
  name: string,
  metadata?: Record<string, any>
}): { projectId: string, created: boolean }
```

#### 2. Register Agent
Registers an agent to receive messages in a project:
```typescript
agentmail_register_agent({
  projectId: string,
  agentId: string,
  capabilities: string[],
  metadata?: Record<string, any>
}): { registered: boolean, mailboxId: string }
```

#### 3. Send Message
Sends a message to another agent's mailbox:
```typescript
agentmail_send_message({
  projectId: string,
  to: string,           // Agent ID
  subject: string,
  body: any,
  priority?: Priority,
  ttl?: number          // Default: 3600 (1 hour)
}): { messageId: string, delivered: boolean }
```

#### 4. Reply
Replies to a received message, maintaining thread context:
```typescript
agentmail_reply({
  messageId: string,    // Original message to reply to
  body: any,
  priority?: Priority
}): { replyId: string, delivered: boolean }
```

#### 5. Fetch Inbox
Retrieves pending messages for an agent:
```typescript
agentmail_fetch_inbox({
  projectId: string,
  agentId: string,
  limit?: number,       // Default: 50
  since?: Date,
  priority?: Priority   // Filter by priority
}): { messages: AgentMailMessage[], hasMore: boolean }
```

#### 6. Request File Reservation
Requests a file reservation through the mail system:
```typescript
agentmail_request_file_reservation({
  projectId: string,
  requesterId: string,
  patterns: string[],   // Glob patterns
  exclusive: boolean,
  duration: number      // Seconds
}): { reservationId: string, granted: boolean, conflicts?: string[] }
```

## Implementation Details

### MCP Server Configuration
```typescript
const mcpServer = new MCPServer({
  name: 'flywheel-agentmail',
  version: '1.0.0',
  capabilities: {
    tools: true,
    resources: false,
    prompts: false
  }
});
```

### Message Queue Backend
- In-memory queue for development
- Redis-backed queue for production
- PostgreSQL for message persistence and history
- Configurable retention policies

### Delivery Guarantees
- At-least-once delivery with idempotency keys
- Dead letter queue for undeliverable messages
- Retry with exponential backoff (max 3 attempts)
- Delivery confirmation via acknowledgment

## File Locations

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `packages/flywheel-clients/src/agentmail/index.ts` | Public exports |
| `packages/flywheel-clients/src/agentmail/mcp-server.ts` | MCP server implementation |
| `packages/flywheel-clients/src/agentmail/tools/` | Individual MCP tool handlers |
| `packages/flywheel-clients/src/agentmail/queue.ts` | Message queue abstraction |
| `packages/flywheel-clients/src/agentmail/types.ts` | TypeScript interfaces |
| `packages/flywheel-clients/src/agentmail/storage/` | Storage adapters (memory, Redis, Postgres) |
| `packages/flywheel-clients/src/agentmail/__tests__/` | Test suites |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] MCP server with all 6 tools implemented
- [ ] Tool input/output schema validation
- [ ] Message persistence across restarts
- [ ] Thread support via replyTo chains
- [ ] Priority queue ordering (urgent > high > normal > low)
- [ ] TTL enforcement with automatic expiration
- [ ] Dead letter queue for failed deliveries
- [ ] At-least-once delivery guarantee
- [ ] Redis adapter for production
- [ ] PostgreSQL adapter for persistence
- [ ] >90% test coverage
- [ ] Integration tests with mock agents
- [ ] Performance: handle 1000 msgs/sec

## References

- PLAN.md Section 10: Inter-Agent Communication
- PLAN.md Section 11: Agent Mail Protocol
- MCP Specification: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/

## Dependencies

- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` - MCP SDK
- `ioredis` - Redis client
- `pg` - PostgreSQL client
- Shared types from `@flywheel/types`

## Security Considerations

- Validate agent identity before message access
- Enforce project-level isolation
- Rate limit message sending (default: 100/min/agent)
- Sanitize message body content
- Encrypt sensitive message content at rest

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-66n","title":"FEAT: Flywheel Velocity Dashboard","description":"## Background

The Flywheel methodology is built on the premise of continuous improvement - each cycle through Plan, Coordinate, Execute, Scan, and Remember should be faster and more effective than the last. The Velocity Dashboard provides a quantitative measure of this improvement, enabling operators to understand how well their Flywheel ecosystem is accelerating and where friction exists.

## Reasoning

Velocity measurement is crucial because:
- **Validates the Flywheel Concept**: Proves that the system is actually improving over time
- **Identifies Bottlenecks**: Highlights which stages are slowing down the cycle
- **Guides Investment**: Shows where improvements will have the most impact
- **Motivates Teams**: Visible progress encourages continued adoption

The velocity score provides a single, intuitive metric (0-100) that captures overall ecosystem health, while detailed stage metrics enable diagnosis and optimization.

## Technical Considerations

### Velocity Score Calculation

**Composite Score (0-100):**
```typescript
interface VelocityScore {
  overall_score: number; // 0-100
  timestamp: Date;
  period: '24h' | '7d' | '30d';
  
  components: {
    throughput_score: number;      // Weight: 25%
    cycle_time_score: number;      // Weight: 25%
    success_rate_score: number;    // Weight: 20%
    learning_rate_score: number;   // Weight: 20%
    collaboration_score: number;   // Weight: 10%
  };
  
  trend: 'accelerating' | 'stable' | 'decelerating';
  trend_magnitude: number; // percentage change
}
```

**Component Calculations:**

1. **Throughput Score (25%):**
   - Tasks completed per hour normalized against capacity
   - Baseline: First 30 days of operation
   - Score: (current_throughput / baseline_throughput) * 50, capped at 100

2. **Cycle Time Score (25%):**
   - Average time from task creation to completion
   - Compared against historical best and baseline
   - Score: (baseline_time / current_time) * 50, capped at 100

3. **Success Rate Score (20%):**
   - Percentage of tasks completing successfully
   - Score: success_rate directly (90% = 90 score)

4. **Learning Rate Score (20%):**
   - Improvement in efficiency over time
   - Measures knowledge reuse and error reduction
   - Calculated from derivative of efficiency metrics

5. **Collaboration Score (10%):**
   - Efficiency of multi-agent coordination
   - Handoff success rate and latency
   - Cross-agent knowledge sharing

### Per-Stage Metrics

**Plan Stage:**
```typescript
interface PlanStageMetrics {
  avg_planning_duration_seconds: number;
  plan_quality_score: number; // based on execution success
  plan_revision_rate: number; // replanning frequency
  estimation_accuracy: number; // predicted vs actual duration
  complexity_assessment_accuracy: number;
}
```

**Coordinate Stage:**
```typescript
interface CoordinateStageMetrics {
  avg_coordination_duration_seconds: number;
  agent_assignment_efficiency: number;
  resource_contention_rate: number;
  parallel_execution_ratio: number;
  coordination_overhead_percent: number;
}
```

**Execute Stage:**
```typescript
interface ExecuteStageMetrics {
  avg_execution_duration_seconds: number;
  tool_call_success_rate: number;
  retry_rate: number;
  context_switch_frequency: number;
  execution_efficiency: number; // useful work / total time
}
```

**Scan Stage:**
```typescript
interface ScanStageMetrics {
  avg_scan_duration_seconds: number;
  files_scanned_per_second: number;
  issue_detection_rate: number;
  false_positive_rate: number;
  scan_coverage_percent: number;
}
```

**Remember Stage:**
```typescript
interface RememberStageMetrics {
  avg_remember_duration_seconds: number;
  knowledge_entries_created: number;
  knowledge_retrieval_hit_rate: number;
  knowledge_freshness_score: number;
  cross_agent_sharing_rate: number;
}
```

### Learning Rate Tracking

**Learning Metrics:**
```typescript
interface LearningMetrics {
  period: DateRange;
  
  improvement_rate: {
    overall: number; // percentage improvement per week
    by_task_type: Map<string, number>;
    by_agent: Map<string, number>;
  };
  
  knowledge_reuse: {
    cache_hit_rate: number;
    similar_task_acceleration: number;
    pattern_recognition_improvement: number;
  };
  
  error_reduction: {
    overall_error_rate_trend: number; // negative = improvement
    recurring_error_elimination: number;
    novel_error_rate: number;
  };
}
```

**Learning Indicators:**
- **Positive Learning**: Decreasing error rates, increasing throughput
- **Knowledge Accumulation**: Growing knowledge base, higher reuse
- **Pattern Recognition**: Faster handling of similar tasks
- **Error Prevention**: Fewer repeated mistakes

### Trend Indicators

**Trend Detection:**
```typescript
interface TrendAnalysis {
  velocity_trend: 'accelerating' | 'stable' | 'decelerating';
  confidence: number;
  
  acceleration_factors: string[]; // What's helping
  deceleration_factors: string[]; // What's hurting
  
  forecast_7d: {
    expected_velocity: number;
    confidence_interval: [number, number];
  };
  
  recommendations: TrendRecommendation[];
}
```

**Trend Thresholds:**
- **Accelerating**: > 5% improvement week-over-week
- **Stable**: -2% to +5% change
- **Decelerating**: < -2% decline

**Trend Visualization:**
- Velocity sparkline with trend line
- Color-coded trend indicator (green/yellow/red)
- Comparison to historical periods
- Anomaly highlighting

### Dashboard Layout

**Main Dashboard Sections:**

1. **Hero Velocity Gauge:**
   - Large circular gauge (0-100)
   - Current score prominently displayed
   - Trend arrow with percentage change
   - Comparison to 30-day average

2. **Stage Performance Strip:**
   - Five horizontal bars for each stage
   - Color-coded performance (vs baseline)
   - Click to drill into stage details
   - Mini sparklines for trends

3. **Learning Rate Panel:**
   - Improvement rate over time chart
   - Knowledge reuse metrics
   - Error reduction trend
   - Key learning milestones

4. **Trend Analysis Section:**
   - Trend status with explanation
   - Acceleration/deceleration factors
   - Actionable recommendations
   - Forecast visualization

5. **Historical Comparison:**
   - Velocity over time (30/60/90 days)
   - Week-over-week comparisons
   - Best/worst day analysis
   - Milestone markers

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Velocity Score**
   - [ ] Composite score calculated from 5 components
   - [ ] Score updates in real-time (< 1 minute lag)
   - [ ] Historical scores stored for trending
   - [ ] Score explanation available on hover

2. **Stage Metrics**
   - [ ] All 5 stages measured independently
   - [ ] Duration, quality, and efficiency per stage
   - [ ] Stage bottleneck identification
   - [ ] Stage-specific recommendations

3. **Learning Rate**
   - [ ] Improvement rate calculated weekly
   - [ ] Knowledge reuse metrics tracked
   - [ ] Error reduction trends visible
   - [ ] Learning milestones highlighted

4. **Trend Analysis**
   - [ ] Trend detected with confidence score
   - [ ] Factors explained in plain language
   - [ ] 7-day forecast with confidence interval
   - [ ] Recommendations for improvement

5. **Dashboard UI**
   - [ ] Velocity gauge as focal point
   - [ ] Stage breakdown with drill-down
   - [ ] Learning metrics visualization
   - [ ] Trend indicators with explanations
   - [ ] Responsive design for all screen sizes

## File Locations

### Backend Services
- `apps/gateway/src/services/velocity.service.ts` - Core velocity calculation
- `apps/gateway/src/services/stage-metrics.service.ts` - Per-stage metrics
- `apps/gateway/src/services/learning-rate.service.ts` - Learning tracking
- `apps/gateway/src/services/trend-analysis.service.ts` - Trend detection
- `apps/gateway/src/controllers/velocity.controller.ts` - Velocity API

### Database
- `packages/database/prisma/migrations/xxx_add_velocity_tracking.sql` - Schema
- Tables: `velocity_scores`, `stage_metrics`, `learning_metrics`, `trend_analysis`

### Frontend Components
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/FlywheelVelocityDashboard.tsx` - Main dashboard
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/VelocityGauge.tsx` - Hero gauge component
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/StagePerformanceStrip.tsx` - Stage breakdown
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/LearningRatePanel.tsx` - Learning metrics
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/TrendIndicator.tsx` - Trend visualization
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/VelocityHistoryChart.tsx` - Historical chart

### Algorithms
- `apps/gateway/src/algorithms/velocity-score.ts` - Score calculation
- `apps/gateway/src/algorithms/trend-detection.ts` - CUSUM/EWMA trend detection
- `apps/gateway/src/algorithms/learning-rate.ts` - Learning curve fitting

## References

- PLAN.md §21.7 - Flywheel Velocity Dashboard
- Flywheel Methodology: Plan -> Coordinate -> Execute -> Scan -> Remember
- Statistical Methods: Exponentially Weighted Moving Average, Change Point Detection


## Testing Requirements

Reference: `flywheel_gateway-d8b`.

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Velocity metrics aggregation is deterministic (same inputs → same output)
- [ ] Scoring/weighting logic handles missing subsystems (partial availability) without NaNs
- [ ] Time-window computations (7d/30d) are correct and timezone-safe

### Integration Tests
- [ ] With stubbed integrations (BV/CASS/Scanner/Metrics): dashboard endpoint returns a complete, validated response

### E2E Tests (UI)
- [ ] Dashboard renders velocity overview and updates when new events arrive

### Logging
- [ ] Logs include correlationId + window + component availability flags + computation latency
